patterns.name: PR Review Squad
patterns.description: Most common pattern — spawns 5-8 specialized reviewer agents in parallel for comprehensive code review. Each agent has a distinct review lens.

patterns.name: Lightweight Recon
patterns.description: Single task using a smaller, faster model for quick reconnaissance and exploration. Prioritizes speed over depth.

patterns.name: Parallel File Reading
patterns.description: Multiple tasks dispatched in parallel to read different files simultaneously. Uses minimal toolsets and often a lighter model.
use_cases: Reading multiple source files at once
Bulk file inspection before an edit

patterns.name: Document / Research Tasks
patterns.description: Single agent with a named persona assigned to a focused document review or research task.
use_cases: Researching a repository's structure or conventions
Analyzing data flows or control flows

patterns.name: Creative / Simple Generation
patterns.description: Single task with an empty tools array, relying purely on LLM generation with no file system or tool access.

key_insights: Parallel code review with named specialist agents is the dominant pattern.
Lighter models (claude-haiku-4-5) are preferred for recon and bulk-read tasks to save cost and latency.
Tool scoping is deliberate — read-only tools for recon, empty tools for pure generation.
Named agents add semantic clarity and allow persona-driven review angles.
Chain mode was not observed in the sampled invocations.
